mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive skew when FEDFUNDS declines over consecutive months (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, tight labor markets compress wage growth margins and signal peak cyclical demand, triggering mean-reverting equity pullbacks.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns remain positive in over 60% of observations where UNRATE stays below 5.0 percent for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and consumer credit indicate a synchronized contraction in household balance sheets, which reduces aggregate demand and depresses corporate revenue expectations.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both decline > 2% month-over-month
failure_condition: Rank IC between the joint HOUST-RSAFS decline signal and 60-day SPX returns exceeds 0.05 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: In periods of rapid industrial production growth, supply chain bottlenecks emerge faster than demand can absorb output, causing inventory gluts that compress profit margins and drag equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:2005 industrial output accelerated post-2004 recovery
failure_condition: Forward 60-day SPX returns are not significantly lower when INDPRO growth exceeds 1.5% quarter-over-quarter compared to baseline periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth expands the earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically supporting higher equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly SPX returns show no positive correlation with sequential GDPC1 growth rates over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5 percent, borrowing costs suppress corporate capital expenditure and consumer durable purchases, leading to downward earnings revisions and negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:2005 rate cycle peaked near 4.25 percent
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns remain positive in more than 55% of trading days when FEDFUNDS stays above 4.5 percent for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong employment growth and stagnant industrial production indicates a services-led economic shift that reduces manufacturing export competitiveness, pressuring multinational S&P 500 earnings.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises while INDPRO falls month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-INDPRO divergence signal fails to predict negative 20-day SPX returns with a statistical significance of p < 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated consumer credit expansion, household leverage reaches unsustainable levels, setting up a sharp consumption drawdown that directly reduces retail and consumer discretionary revenues.
evidence_refs: fact:2005 consumer credit growth accelerated to 8% annualized
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns do not exhibit a statistically significant negative skew when RSAFS growth exceeds 7% year-over-year.
